identity.name: AI Access — High-Volume Gemini Prompt Burst
identity.summary: A single user fires a burst of prompts at the Google Gemini API. The llm_provider_egress plugin POSTs 30 sequential requests to generativelanguage.googleapis.com within ~90s; Cortex AI Access Security profiles the per-user request rate against a rolling baseline and the NGFW counts sustained generative-AI sessions from one source IP. Gemini is classified as unsanctioned for this user.
identity.description: Anomalous AI provider usage volume is an early indicator of bulk data exposure, automated scraping or runaway agent loops. This scenario emits 30 sequential POSTs to generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-1.5-flash:generateContent from one user within roughly 90 seconds, then — after a cool-off window — a single probe to confirm the baseline restores and no alert fires. Detection is volume-based: (1) an analytics rule profiles per-user request count to each provider over a rolling window and alerts when it exceeds baseline; (2) the NGFW counts sustained generative-AI sessions from a single source IP; (3) a sanctioned-vs-unsanctioned policy classifies Gemini as unsanctioned for this user. The cool-off probe is the negative control — its single request stays below baseline and produces no detection. A correlation rule groups the burst signals into one anomalous-usage incident keyed on the user identity.

prerequisites.description: Cortex AI Access Security licensed with per-user volume baselining enabled.
Lab environment matching scenario SIM-AIACC-003 requirements (egress allowed to generativelanguage.googleapis.com).

payload.code:
# step-1: Pre-flight DNS resolution for generativelanguage.googleapis.com
getent hosts generativelanguage.googleapis.com || true
echo "[CortexSim] burst target reachable"
# step-2: Fire 30 sequential prompts to trigger volume baseline
cat > /tmp/aiacc-003.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-003
name: "AI Access — Gemini burst"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - generativelanguage.googleapis.com
dry_run: false
tags: [ai-access, gemini, volume-anomaly]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: gemini
      payload_type: benign
      iterations: 30
      sleep_seconds: 2
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-003.yml --live
# step-3: Cool-off window then a single probe to confirm baseline restoration
sleep 120
cat > /tmp/aiacc-003-cool.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-003
name: "AI Access — Gemini cool-off probe"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - generativelanguage.googleapis.com
dry_run: false
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: gemini
      payload_type: benign
      iterations: 1
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-003-cool.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: 30 outbound HTTPS POSTs to generativelanguage.googleapis.com within ~90s from one source.

xql_queries.name: AIACC-003 Pre-flight Gemini Provider Host Resolvable
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com" or tls_sni = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
| comp count() as sessions by src_ip
| filter sessions >= 1
| fields src_ip, sessions

xql_queries.name: AIACC-003 Gemini Request Rate Exceeds Per User Baseline
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains ":generateContent"
| comp count() as request_count, sum(bytes_sent) as total_bytes by source_user, src_ip
| filter request_count >= 20
| fields source_user, src_ip, request_count, total_bytes

xql_queries.name: AIACC-003 NGFW Sustained Generative AI Session Count Single Source
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com" or tls_sni = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
| filter app in ("google-gemini", "gemini-api", "generative-ai", "ai-ml-application")
| comp count() as session_count by src_ip, app
| filter session_count >= 20
| fields src_ip, app, session_count

xql_queries.name: AIACC-003 Unsanctioned AI Provider Gemini For User
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
| filter app_category in ("generative-ai", "ai-ml-application") and app_sanctioned = false
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, app, app_sanctioned, action

xql_queries.name: AIACC-003 Post Cooloff Single Gemini Request Below Baseline
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 300)
| filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains ":generateContent"
| comp count() as request_count by source_user, src_ip
| filter request_count <= 3
| fields source_user, src_ip, request_count

correlation_rules.name: Anomalous Gemini Usage Volume Single User
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(AIACC-003 Gemini Request Rate Exceeds Per User Baseline) OR XQL(AIACC-003 NGFW Sustained Generative AI Session Count Single Source) OR XQL(AIACC-003 Unsanctioned AI Provider Gemini For User)

analytics_modules: Cortex AI Access Security — Per-user provider request-rate baselining
Cortex AI Access Security — Sanctioned-vs-unsanctioned provider classification

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_host = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com" | comp count() as request_count by source_user | filter request_count >= 20 | fields source_user, request_count
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app in ("google-gemini","gemini-api","generative-ai") | comp count() as sessions by src_ip | filter sessions >= 20
Correlation CR-AIACC-0003 groups the per-user rate, NGFW session count and unsanctioned-provider signals into one anomalous-usage incident.

use_cases.name: Anomalous AI Provider Usage Volume
use_cases.description: Detect a single user bursting prompts at an unsanctioned AI provider above their rolling baseline and confirm the baseline restores after cool-off.

test_cases.objective: A 30-request burst to Gemini fires the per-user volume-anomaly rule.
preconditions: Cortex AI Access Security inline with per-user volume baselining enabled.
Gemini classified as unsanctioned for the test user.
steps: Observe the volume-anomaly alert in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `AIACC-003 Gemini Request Rate Exceeds Per User Baseline` returns >= 1 row.
XQL `AIACC-003 NGFW Sustained Generative AI Session Count Single Source` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: After cool-off, a single request stays below baseline and produces no alert.
success_criteria: XQL `AIACC-003 Post Cooloff Single Gemini Request Below Baseline` returns >= 1 row.
No new volume-anomaly alert fires for the cool-off probe.

preventive_controls: Apply per-user rate limits on generative-AI App-IDs via the NGFW and Cortex AI Access Security.
Classify approved providers as sanctioned and block or throttle unsanctioned ones.
Alert and require justification when a user crosses their usage baseline.
detection_engineering: Baseline per-user request counts to each provider and alert on bursts above the rolling window.
Count sustained generative-AI sessions per source IP on the NGFW as a corroborating signal.
Use a negative-control probe after cool-off to validate the baseline restores cleanly.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK T1567 — Exfiltration Over Web Service
